# UC-19.2.27 · vSAN Disk Group Capacity Headroom and Mount State

## Description

Disk groups nearing full capacity slow resyncs and increase rebuild time, which extends windows of reduced fault tolerance. Monitoring per–disk group free space and mount state prevents surprise admission control during failures.

## Value

Disk groups nearing full capacity slow resyncs and increase rebuild time, which extends windows of reduced fault tolerance. Monitoring per–disk group free space and mount state prevents surprise admission control during failures.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest vSAN disk group metrics from RVC or vSAN SDK exporter; (2) warn at 80% used and critical at 90%; (3) page on any disk group not mounted; (4) correlate with physical disk SMART (UC-19.2.5).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: VMware vSAN health via vCenter TA or custom API collector.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hci` `sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup"` with fields `cluster`, `host`, `dg_name`, `used_pct`, `state`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest vSAN disk group metrics from RVC or vSAN SDK exporter; (2) warn at 80% used and critical at 90%; (3) page on any disk group not mounted; (4) correlate with physical disk SMART (UC-19.2.5).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-1h
| stats latest(used_pct) as used_pct, latest(state) as st by cluster, host, dg_name
| where used_pct>80 OR st!="mounted"
| sort -used_pct
| table cluster, host, dg_name, used_pct, st
```

Understanding this SPL

**vSAN Disk Group Capacity Headroom and Mount State** — Disk groups nearing full capacity slow resyncs and increase rebuild time, which extends windows of reduced fault tolerance. Monitoring per–disk group free space and mount state prevents surprise admission control during failures.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hci` `sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup"` with fields `cluster`, `host`, `dg_name`, `used_pct`, `state`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): VMware vSAN health via vCenter TA or custom API collector. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: vsan:diskgroup. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster, host, dg_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where used_pct>80 OR st!="mounted"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **vSAN Disk Group Capacity Headroom and Mount State**): table cluster, host, dg_name, used_pct, st


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with vSphere and vSAN health UIs, Skyline, or VMware vCenter for the same objects and time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (used % per DG), Table (critical disk groups), Heatmap (host × DG utilization).

## SPL

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-1h
| stats latest(used_pct) as used_pct, latest(state) as st by cluster, host, dg_name
| where used_pct>80 OR st!="mounted"
| sort -used_pct
| table cluster, host, dg_name, used_pct, st
```

## Visualization

Gauge (used % per DG), Table (critical disk groups), Heatmap (host × DG utilization).
